summary: Pterocarya macroptera, the large-winged wingnut, is a species of Pterocarya native to the temperate parts of China. It can be found in much of North Central, South Central and Southeast China as well as in the Tibetan area. On average, a tree grows to around 15-30 meters (49.2-98.4 ft). The species belongs to the section Platyptera within the genus Pterocarya, which also includes P. rhoifolia, P.insignis and P. delavayi. Some scientists consider P. insignis and P. delavayi to be varieties of Pterocarya macroptera. The species is vulnerable.
